# Subjecto

> Minimalistic, zero-dependency TypeScript state management library. Two primitives: Subject<T> (flat reactive values) and DeepSubject<T> (proxy-based nested object observation with path subscriptions).

## Install

npm install subjecto

## Entry Points

| Import path | What you get | Size (gzip) |
|---|---|---|
| `subjecto` | Subject + DeepSubject + batch + helpers | 1.8KB |
| `subjecto/core` | Subject only | 0.6KB |
| `subjecto/helpers` | nextAssign, nextPush, toggle, once, complete | 0.3KB |
| `subjecto/react` | useSubject, useDeepSubject, useDeepSubjectSelector, Paths, PathValue types | 0.5KB |
| `subjecto/debug` | debugSubject (dev-only visual UI) | 7KB |

## Subject<T> — flat reactive value

```typescript
import { Subject } from 'subjecto'

// Create
const count = new Subject<number>(0)
const user = new Subject({ name: 'Alice', age: 30 }, { name: 'user' })

// Subscribe (callback fires immediately with current value)
const handle = count.subscribe((value) => console.log(value))

// Update
count.next(1)

// Read
count.getValue() // 1

// Unsubscribe
handle.unsubscribe()

// Unsubscribe all
count.complete()
```

### Subject methods

```typescript
subject.next(value: T): void                    // Set new value, notify subscribers
subject.getValue(): T                            // Get current value
subject.subscribe(cb: (v: T) => void): handle   // Subscribe; cb called immediately with current value
subject.unsubscribe(id: symbol): boolean         // Unsubscribe by ID
subject.complete(): void                         // Remove all subscribers
subject.nextAssign(partial: Partial<T>): void    // Object.assign merge (throws if not object)
subject.nextPush(item: unknown): void            // Array push (throws if not array)
subject.toggle(): void                           // Flip boolean (throws if not boolean)
subject.once(cb: (v: T) => void): void           // Subscribe once, auto-unsubscribe after first call
```

### Subject properties

```typescript
subject.subscribers    // Map<symbol, callback> — all active subscriptions
subject.count          // number — update count (starts at 1)
subject.debug          // boolean | ((v: T) => void) — enable debug logging (dev only)
subject.before         // (v: T) => T — transform value before setting
subject.options        // { name?: string, updateIfStrictlyEqual?: boolean }
```

### Constructor options

```typescript
new Subject<T>(initialValue: T, options?: {
  name?: string                    // Debug name (default: 'noName')
  updateIfStrictlyEqual?: boolean  // Notify even if value === prev (default: true)
})
```

## DeepSubject<T> — proxy-based nested observation

```typescript
import { DeepSubject } from 'subjecto'

const state = new DeepSubject({
  user: { name: 'Alice', age: 30 },
  cart: { items: [], total: 0 },
})

// Subscribe to a path (callback fires immediately)
const handle = state.subscribe('user/name', (name) => console.log(name))

// Mutate through proxy — triggers subscribers automatically
state.getValue().user.name = 'Bob'

// Array mutations trigger subscribers and produce a new array reference (React-compatible)
state.getValue().cart.items.push({ id: 1, price: 10 })

// Unsubscribe
handle.unsubscribe()

// Unsubscribe all
state.complete()
```

### Path patterns

```
"user/name"     — exact path
"user/*"        — any direct child of user
"user/**"       — any descendant of user at any depth
"**"            — all changes everywhere
```

### DeepSubject methods

```typescript
state.subscribe(pattern: string, cb: (v: unknown) => void, opts?): handle
state.getValue(): T                  // Returns proxied object — mutations trigger subscriptions
state.next(newValue: T): void        // Replace entire state
state.complete(): void               // Remove all subscribers
state.unsubscribe(cb): void          // Remove a specific callback from all patterns
```

### Subscribe options

```typescript
state.subscribe('path', callback, { skipInitialCall: true })
// skipInitialCall: don't fire callback immediately (useful for React integration)
```

## batch() — coalesce mutations

```typescript
import { batch } from 'subjecto'

// Without batch: two notification cycles
state.getValue().user.name = 'Bob'
state.getValue().user.age = 31

// With batch: one notification cycle, deduplicated per path
batch(() => {
  state.getValue().user.name = 'Bob'
  state.getValue().user.age = 31
})

// Nested batches flush only when outermost completes
```

## React Hooks

Requires React >= 18 (peer dependency, optional).

### useSubject

```tsx
import { Subject } from 'subjecto'
import { useSubject } from 'subjecto/react'

// Create OUTSIDE components
const countSubject = new Subject(0)

function Counter() {
  const [count, setCount] = useSubject(countSubject)
  return <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>{count}</button>
}
```

### useDeepSubject — type-safe path subscription

```tsx
import { DeepSubject } from 'subjecto'
import { useDeepSubject } from 'subjecto/react'

const appState = new DeepSubject({
  user: { name: 'Alice', profile: { bio: 'Engineer' } },
  cart: { items: [] as Array<{ id: number; price: number }> },
})

function UserName() {
  // TypeScript infers [string, (v: string) => void]
  const [name, setName] = useDeepSubject(appState, 'user/name')
  return <button onClick={() => setName('Bob')}>{name}</button>
}

function Profile() {
  // TypeScript infers [{ bio: string }, setter]
  const [profile] = useDeepSubject(appState, 'user/profile')
  return <p>{profile.bio}</p>
}
```

### useDeepSubjectSelector — derived values

```tsx
import { useDeepSubjectSelector } from 'subjecto/react'

function CartTotal() {
  const total = useDeepSubjectSelector(
    appState,
    'cart/items',
    (items) => items.reduce((sum, item) => sum + item.price, 0),
  )
  return <p>Total: ${total}</p>
}

// Custom equality function (default: shallow equality)
const count = useDeepSubjectSelector(
  appState, 'cart/items',
  (items) => items.length,
  (a, b) => a === b,
)
```

### React type utilities

```typescript
import type { Paths, PathValue } from 'subjecto/react'

interface AppState {
  user: { name: string; age: number }
  cart: { items: Array<{ id: number }> }
}

type P = Paths<AppState>
// "user" | "user/name" | "user/age" | "cart" | "cart/items"

type V = PathValue<AppState, 'user/name'>
// string
```

## Tree-shakeable helpers

Standalone functions that delegate to Subject methods. Import from `subjecto/helpers` for smallest bundle, or from `subjecto` for convenience.

```typescript
import { nextAssign, nextPush, toggle, once, complete } from 'subjecto/helpers'

const obj = new Subject({ a: 1, b: 2 })
nextAssign(obj, { b: 3 })  // { a: 1, b: 3 }

const arr = new Subject([1, 2])
nextPush(arr, 3)            // [1, 2, 3]

const flag = new Subject(false)
toggle(flag)                // true

const s = new Subject(0)
once(s, (v) => console.log(v))  // fires once, then auto-unsubscribes

complete(s)                 // removes all subscribers
```

## Common Mistakes

WRONG: Creating subjects inside React components (recreated every render)
```tsx
// BAD
function App() {
  const subject = new Subject(0)  // new instance every render!
  const [val] = useSubject(subject)
}
```

RIGHT: Create subjects outside components
```tsx
const subject = new Subject(0)
function App() {
  const [val] = useSubject(subject)
}
```

---

WRONG: Importing React hooks from wrong path
```tsx
import { useSubject } from 'subjecto'  // does NOT export hooks
```

RIGHT:
```tsx
import { useSubject } from 'subjecto/react'
```

---

WRONG: Mutating DeepSubject value without going through proxy
```tsx
const raw = { ...state.getValue() }
raw.user.name = 'Bob'  // no proxy — subscribers NOT notified
```

RIGHT: Mutate through getValue() proxy
```tsx
state.getValue().user.name = 'Bob'  // proxy intercepts — subscribers notified
```

---

WRONG: Subscribing to array indices
```tsx
state.subscribe('cart/items/0', cb)  // does NOT work
```

RIGHT: Subscribe to the array itself
```tsx
state.subscribe('cart/items', cb)
```

---

WRONG: toggle() on non-boolean (throws Error)
```tsx
const s = new Subject('hello')
s.toggle()  // throws: "Subject value must be a boolean"
```

---

NOTE: subscribe() calls the callback immediately with the current value.
Use { skipInitialCall: true } if you don't want this (DeepSubject only).

NOTE: Paths use forward slashes, not dots: "user/name" not "user.name"

NOTE: Circular references in DeepSubject are safe (proxy cache prevents infinite recursion).

NOTE: DeepSubject array methods (push, pop, splice, sort, reverse, shift, unshift) produce a new array reference after mutation. This ensures React deps (useMemo, useEffect) and Object.is comparisons detect changes correctly.

- [API source: React hooks](https://github.com/paulbrie/subjecto/blob/master/src/react/useDeepSubject.ts)
- [API source: Helpers](https://github.com/paulbrie/subjecto/blob/master/src/helpers.ts)
- [API source: Types](https://github.com/paulbrie/subjecto/blob/master/src/react/types.ts)
